title: I Roommate

jokes.text: I was up really late poking through people's desks.
jokes.setup: Fry apologizes for being late to work
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was up late doing something invasive and inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's invasive tendencies, solid structure with misdirection

jokes.text: Somebody's been leaving food around and it's attracting owls. And I, for one, am getting tired of cleaning those owl traps.
jokes.setup: Discussion of pest problem in the office
jokes.punchline: The pests are owls, and they have owl traps
jokes.explanation: Classic Futurama absurdity - owls as pests is unexpected and funny, good world-building

jokes.text: I notice Fry has been here for a month so I'm appointing him head of a committee to find who's responsible.
jokes.setup: Water consumption has tripled, Fry arrived a month ago
jokes.punchline: Professor appoints the obvious culprit to investigate himself
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup with clear logical gap that the Professor misses

jokes.text: Am I cracking up or is Fry's living here starting to get in the way of bus...
jokes.setup: Hermes starts to voice concerns about Fry
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off mid-word, highlighting the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Simple interruption gag, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You're getting a huge dose of radiation. And great lift.
jokes.setup: Bender asks what Fry is doing near dangerous engines
jokes.punchline: Adds 'and great lift' as if radiation exposure has beauty benefits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line - finds the silver lining in radiation poisoning with robot logic

jokes.text: Hey, when you look this good, you don't have to know anything.
jokes.setup: Fry is getting irradiated but claims it's worth it for appearance
jokes.punchline: Fry thinks he looks good despite being a disheveled slob
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry delusion - his confidence is completely misplaced given his appearance

jokes.text: That's right! We want some money!
jokes.setup: Leela says they need to talk about Fry
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately assumes it's about money before hearing the actual issue
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment - his greed makes him jump to the wrong conclusion

jokes.text: I seem to have mislaid my alien mummy.
jokes.setup: Professor dismisses their concerns, says he's busy
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions losing an ancient alien mummy like it's car keys
jokes.explanation: Excellent understated delivery of something absurd - 'mislaid' is perfect word choice

jokes.text: Hey, Professor. Mmm. Great jerky.
jokes.setup: Professor explains the valuable ancient mummy in the sarcophagus
jokes.punchline: Fry has been eating it as jerky
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff with great timing - the casual 'great jerky' after the elaborate historical description is brilliant

jokes.text: My God, this is an outrage. I was going to eat that mummy.
jokes.setup: Professor is outraged that Fry ate the ancient mummy
jokes.punchline: Not because it's historically valuable, but because he wanted to eat it himself
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - takes the absurd situation and makes it even more absurd with the Professor's priorities

jokes.text: Sheesh. 40,000 channels and only 150 of them have anything good on.
jokes.setup: Fry is channel surfing in the future
jokes.punchline: Even with 40,000 channels, he can only find 150 good ones
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the 'nothing on TV' complaint to absurd future proportions

jokes.text: Metric? I've always known. But for you, my darling, I'm willing to convert.
jokes.setup: Robot soap opera proposal scene with ring fitting
jokes.punchline: Makes measurement system conversion sound romantic
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - robot romance with measurement pun

jokes.text: Hmm, must be a friend of theirs.
jokes.setup: Camera pans to show Fry watching the robot soap opera
jokes.punchline: Fry assumes the human character must be someone the robots know personally
jokes.explanation: Mild character comedy showing Fry doesn't understand how TV shows work

jokes.text: No, Fry, by 'close quarters' I mean this office. Uh-huh. And by 'people,' I mean you. Right. And by 'inconsiderate,' I mean... Leela, we're trying to watch TV.
jokes.setup: Leela tries to diplomatically explain Fry's problems
jokes.punchline: Fry interrupts her explanation to demonstrate the exact behavior she's criticizing
jokes.explanation: Great ironic timing - Fry demonstrates the inconsiderate behavior while being told about it

jokes.text: That's a human. What's he do? Usual human stuff. He laughs, he learns, he loves. Boring.
jokes.setup: Robots in the TV show discuss the human character
jokes.punchline: Human activities are considered boring to robot TV audience
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on human nature from robot perspective

jokes.text: Hey, uh, why is the TV getting smaller?
jokes.setup: Everyone is focused on the TV drama
jokes.punchline: Fry notices the TV physically shrinking as they're repossessing it
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag with good timing - Fry's oblivious observation

jokes.text: We'll bill you for the couch.
jokes.setup: Repo men take the TV
jokes.punchline: Also charge for damage to furniture they caused during repossession
jokes.explanation: Standard repo humor - adds insult to injury but not particularly original

jokes.text: Nah, I'm trying to watch my input. I need plenty of wholesome, nutritious alcohol.
jokes.setup: Being offered food
jokes.punchline: Robot calling alcohol wholesome and nutritious while 'watching his input'
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific humor playing with diet/health concepts applied to robot physiology.

jokes.text: You barely touched your amoeba. You want it? Nah, I'm trying to watch my input. I need plenty of wholesome, nutritious alcohol.
jokes.setup: Bender offers to eat Fry's uneaten food
jokes.punchline: Bender calls alcohol 'wholesome and nutritious' while watching his input
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - treats alcohol like health food

jokes.text: What are the cigars for? They make me look cool.
jokes.setup: Fry asks about Bender's cigars
jokes.punchline: Bender's honest, simple answer about appearance
jokes.explanation: Good Bender honesty - no pretense about sophistication, just wants to look cool

jokes.text: Yeah, but everybody's a jerk... you, me, this jerk. That's my philosophy.
jokes.setup: Fry feels bad about being thrown out for acting like a jerk
jokes.punchline: Bender's nihilistic but oddly comforting philosophy includes random passerby
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender cynicism that's somehow supportive - points at random person as example

jokes.text: Do refrigerators still come in cardboard boxes? Yeah, but the rents are outrageous.
jokes.setup: Fry asks about living in appliance boxes
jokes.punchline: Even cardboard box housing has been commodified in the future
jokes.explanation: Great future dystopia joke - takes homeless stereotype and adds capitalist absurdity

jokes.text: Y-You sure I won't be imposing? Nah. I've always wanted a pet.
jokes.setup: Fry worries about imposing on Bender as roommate
jokes.punchline: Bender sees him as a pet, not an equal roommate
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic setup - establishes Bender's condescending view of humans

jokes.text: This is the TV area... that over there is the breakfast nook... and over here is where you'll be living... which is great, 'cause till now it's just been wasted space.
jokes.setup: Bender gives Fry a tour of his spacious apartment
jokes.punchline: Fry's 'room' is clearly a tiny closet that was indeed wasted space
jokes.explanation: Standard visual gag about tiny living space - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Look, pal, you've only got one set of clothes... and you're not taking them off while I'm here.
jokes.setup: Fry complains about not having room to hang clothes
jokes.punchline: Bender points out the logical flaw and adds uncomfortable boundary
jokes.explanation: Decent Bender logic with mild discomfort humor

jokes.text: Kill all humans. Kill all humans.
jokes.setup: Bender goes to sleep
jokes.punchline: Sleep-talks about killing all humans while his human roommate listens
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender moment - dark humor made awkward by Fry's presence. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Bath what? Bathroom. What room? Bathroom. What, what?
jokes.setup: Fry needs to use the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Bender literally doesn't understand the concept
jokes.explanation: Good robot logic - Bender has no frame of reference for biological needs

jokes.text: Hey, sexy mama... want to kill all humans?
jokes.setup: Bender sees an attractive robot
jokes.punchline: His pickup line is asking if she wants to commit genocide
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that turns his dark impulses into a pickup line - very quotable

jokes.text: You know, beneath his warm, sunny exterior... beats the cold, mechanical heart of a robot.
jokes.setup: Leela comments on how cheerful Bender seems
jokes.punchline: Reverses the usual 'cold exterior, warm heart' cliche
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of a common phrase that works perfectly for robot character

jokes.text: He put in carpeting yesterday and now my head hits the ceiling.
jokes.setup: Fry looks disheveled and explains what happened
jokes.punchline: The 'carpeting' raised the floor so much he can't stand up straight
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing the impracticality of the tiny living space

jokes.text: Hey, do you realize you're standing at a weird angle? Now you're okay.
jokes.setup: Amy notices Fry's odd posture
jokes.punchline: He's been permanently bent from hitting the ceiling - straightens when pointed out
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy callback - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: My place is two cubic meters... and we only take up 1.5 cubic meters. We've got room for a whole 'nother two-thirds of a person.
jokes.setup: Fry says there's not enough room
jokes.punchline: Bender's robot logic calculates precise space measurements including fractional people
jokes.explanation: Good robot logic humor - precise mathematical approach to living space

jokes.text: I've got a lot of great memories in my old place. And now they're gone.
jokes.setup: Bender hesitates about moving, mentions great memories
jokes.punchline: Immediately deletes them without sentimentality
jokes.explanation: Excellent robot character moment - treats memories like disposable computer files

jokes.text: but if you like dank... forget about it.
jokes.setup: Showing underground apartment that's not fancy
jokes.punchline: Sells 'dank' as a positive feature
jokes.explanation: Standard real estate agent humor - spin negative as positive

jokes.text: 'Scuse me. I got to go change a lightbulb.
jokes.setup: Showing apartment with 'great view'
jokes.punchline: Exterminator needs to deal with the owl problem mentioned earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier owl joke - shows consistency in world-building

jokes.text: Hmm, I'm not sure we want to pay... for a dimension we're not going to use.
jokes.setup: Looking at spacious apartment
jokes.punchline: Bender treats 3D space like an unnecessary premium feature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - treating spatial dimensions like cable TV packages

jokes.text: Well, I give up... what's the catch? Oh, no catch. Although we are technically in New Jersey.
jokes.setup: Fry asks about the catch for nice apartment
jokes.punchline: Being in New Jersey is treated as worse than any other problem
jokes.explanation: Standard New Jersey joke - predictable but gets the job done

jokes.text: To shreds, you say. Well, how is his wife holding up? To shreds, you say.
jokes.setup: Professor gets news about colleague Dr. Mobutu
jokes.punchline: Repeats 'to shreds' for both victim and wife with same casual tone
jokes.explanation: Iconic Futurama moment - dark humor with perfect repetitive structure. Extremely quotable.

jokes.text: Was his apartment rent controlled?
jokes.setup: Professor announces Dr. Mobutu's gruesome death
jokes.punchline: Leela's first concern is real estate opportunity
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy - pragmatic response to tragedy shows character priorities

jokes.text: It's not crap. Dr. Mobutu collected this crap... while he was exploring the whatchacallit... universe.
jokes.setup: Fry calls Dr. Mobutu's collection crap
jokes.punchline: Professor defends it by also calling it crap, forgets the word 'universe'
jokes.explanation: Good Professor character moment - absent-minded defense that undermines itself

jokes.text: It's got a TV, you young whatchacallit... idiot.
jokes.setup: Fry complains apartment has no TV
jokes.punchline: Professor forgets the word for 'young person' but remembers 'idiot'
jokes.explanation: Nice Professor moment - his insults are more memorable than his vocabulary

jokes.text: I can barely move. It's perfect!
jokes.setup: Looking at cramped apartment
jokes.punchline: Bender loves what would be uncomfortable for humans
jokes.explanation: Good robot logic - what's uncomfortable for humans is ideal for him

jokes.text: You know, Fry, of all the friends I've had... you're the first.
jokes.setup: Bender seems to be giving heartfelt speech about friendship
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's never had a friend before
jokes.explanation: Sweet misdirection that reveals Bender's loneliness while maintaining his character

jokes.text: This is for you, Fry... Zevulon the great. He's teriyaki-style.
jokes.setup: Amy brings a housewarming gift
jokes.punchline: It's a live alien that she describes like food
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - treating alien life as consumable product

jokes.text: Wow. Heavy-drinking crowd.
jokes.setup: Fry notices his party guests
jokes.punchline: Assumes everyone drinks heavily instead of realizing only Bender needs alcohol
jokes.explanation: Mild misunderstanding humor - Fry misreads the situation

jokes.text: I don't mean to pry into what you're doing in here... with the crab and the one-eyed lady and the Chinese girl... but everyone on this floor is having trouble with their TV reception.
jokes.setup: Neighbor comes to complain about TV interference
jokes.punchline: Describes the main characters in the most superficial way possible
jokes.explanation: Good outsider perspective - reduces main characters to basic physical traits

jokes.text: Wow, that lady's got a huge ass. Those could be anyone's thoughts, fat-ass.
jokes.setup: Neighbors complain Bender's thoughts are being broadcast
jokes.punchline: Bender proves it's him by immediately insulting the complainer
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment - denies responsibility while proving guilt

jokes.text: Well, you were going to live in the closet anyway. Won't you be just as happy back in your old place?
jokes.setup: Fry tries to convince Bender to move out
jokes.punchline: Uses terrible logic that living in a closet is the same as living alone
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Fry's selfish rationalization is transparently bad

jokes.text: Happy. Yeah, that's Bender. Always happy.
jokes.setup: Fry says this arrangement will make them both happy
jokes.punchline: Bender's bitter sarcastic response shows he's clearly not happy
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response showing Bender's hurt feelings

jokes.text: Calculon! You're alive!
jokes.setup: They were mourning Calculon's death
jokes.punchline: He suddenly appears alive, typical soap opera resurrection
jokes.explanation: Standard soap opera parody - resurrection is classic trope

jokes.text: Don't girl me with that girl stuff. Bender and me are guys. Guys don't have feelings.
jokes.setup: Leela tries to explain Bender's hurt feelings
jokes.punchline: Fry's toxic masculinity prevents him from acknowledging emotions
jokes.explanation: Good character flaw moment - Fry's emotional immaturity creates problems

jokes.text: My roommate's gone, and all he left behind was... an eyelash and three skin flakes.
jokes.setup: Bender mourns Fry's departure
jokes.punchline: Counts the gross physical remnants like precious mementos
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor that shows Bender's attachment in robot terms

jokes.text: You've been up all night not drinking, haven't you?
jokes.setup: Bender looking terrible
jokes.punchline: Sobriety being treated as self-destructive behavior for robots
jokes.explanation: Excellent reversal of addiction concerns applied to robot physiology.

jokes.text: Hey... what I don't do is none of your business.
jokes.setup: Leela points out he's been up all night not drinking
jokes.punchline: Bender treats not drinking like a private vice
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal - makes sobriety sound illicit for a robot who needs alcohol

jokes.text: I hate the people who love me, and they hate me.
jokes.setup: Leela mentioning people who love him
jokes.punchline: Bender's circular logic of mutual hatred
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender nihilism with internal logic that makes no sense but sounds profound.

jokes.text: Are you crazy?! That's little Bender you're talking about. I can't cut it off.
jokes.setup: Leela suggests removing his antenna
jokes.punchline: Bender refers to his antenna like a body part with childlike nickname
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's attachment to his anatomy

jokes.text: You're not a robot or a man, so you wouldn't understand.
jokes.setup: Leela tries to give advice about his antenna
jokes.punchline: Bender excludes her from understanding based on gender/species
jokes.explanation: Good Bender logic - creates arbitrary exclusions for emotional advice

jokes.text: You're blind stinking sober.
jokes.setup: Fry finds Bender in dangerous state
jokes.punchline: Reverses 'blind stinking drunk' to fit robot's opposite problem
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that perfectly fits the robot character - very quotable

jokes.text: It's not like he's a ham radio or something.
jokes.setup: Fry dismisses Bender's antenna as useless
jokes.punchline: Compares robot to another electronic device casually
jokes.explanation: Decent dehumanizing humor - treats Bender like comparable technology

jokes.text: Don't help him mutilate himself. But it's a useless antenna. It's not like he's a ham radio or something.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to prevent Bender from self-harm
jokes.punchline: Fry's completely missing the point with literal thinking
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Leela's empathy and Fry's literal robot thinking.

jokes.text: I'm filled with a large number of powerful emotions.
jokes.setup: Emotional soap opera reconciliation scene
jokes.punchline: Robot describes feelings in technical, quantified terms
jokes.explanation: Good robot character humor - emotions described like computer processes

jokes.text: I guess I'm just going to have to get used to being half a robot.
jokes.setup: Bender cut off his antenna to solve the problem
jokes.punchline: Considers himself diminished without his antenna
jokes.explanation: Good pathos moment showing Bender's self-sacrifice for friendship

jokes.text: You call that an antenna?
jokes.setup: They bring Bender's antenna to be reattached
jokes.punchline: Implies what Bender cut off was comically small or inadequate
jokes.explanation: Standard size joke - functional but predictable punchline

jokes.text: In a closet? Oh, humans.
jokes.setup: Fry suggests living in the closet instead
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive comment about human living standards
jokes.explanation: Good ending character moment - Bender's condescending but affectionate view of humans

tmdb_overview: When it is discovered that Philip has been living in the Planet Express office, he is forced to move out. Trying to sleep standing up in Bender's closet-sized apartment doesn't work, so they go looking for a place both can be comfortable. Unfortunately, Bender is incompatible with the ideal apartment they do find until he has a radical... modification which makes him less than happy.
